first_question.text: A latency-sensitive analytics workload reads ~10 TB/day of immutable Parquet files written by an ingest pipeline. The team queries with Spark on Dataproc Serverless from one region, but other teams in different regions also need occasional reads. They want low cost and predictable latency for the primary region while still allowing cross-region access without manually copying data. Which storage configuration fits best?
options: Multi-region Standard bucket co-located with the primary region
Dual-region Standard bucket with the primary region as one of the two regions, using turbo replication
Regional Coldline bucket in the primary region only
Regional Standard bucket in the primary region; teams in other regions transfer copies via Storage Transfer Service
